subject: pigeons
one_liner: The pairing app for pigeons who are done circling the same square and ready to commit to one ledge.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan corporate relationship-app copy applied to feral city pigeons with total sincerity. Hinge's 'designed to be deleted' ethos plays completely straight against the fact that pigeons already mate for life. Never wink. Every number (coo-match percentage, nest-readiness score, flight radius) is stated with the confidence of a real dating app's growth deck. The bird is a serious adult with a route home and standards.

hero.h1: The pigeon you'll share a ledge with is two overpasses away.
hero.sub: Loft pairs pigeons by homing radius, verified coo, and mutual attachment to the same statue. Built to be deleted, unless you lose the nest and have to reinstall.

features.title: Coo Verification
features.blurb: Every profile is authenticated by a three-second coo sample. No doves posing as pigeons, no gulls in from the coast. If the vocalization doesn't clear our reference range, the account never goes live.

features.title: Homing Radius Matching
features.blurb: We only surface birds inside your natural return-flight range, so an evening out never means abandoning the route that gets you home before dark. Distance is measured in wingbeats, not miles.

features.title: Nest-Readiness Score
features.blurb: A 1-to-10 rating of how prepared each bird is to commit to a shared gutter, weighted on twig-gathering history, prior ledge tenure, and how they behave around a dropped bagel. Updated hourly.

pricing_tiers.name: Free Range
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the pigeon still deciding which overpass counts as home.
perks: See who pecked your profile, blurred

pricing_tiers.name: Homebound
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the bird that already knows the ledge it wants.
perks: Undo one accidental peck per day
Priority placement near the breadcrumb feed

pricing_tiers.name: Life Pair
pricing_tiers.tagline: For pigeons ready to delete the app and actually mean it.
perks: One human-assisted introduction on a windowsill

testimonials.quote: Circled the same fountain for four years. Matched with a hen from the parking structure two blocks down. We deleted Loft in March and split a soft pretzel.
testimonials.name: Gerald
testimonials.title: Feral Rock Dove, Financial District Flock

testimonials.quote: The coo verification caught three doves trying to pass themselves off as pigeons. That is vetting I could not have done myself from a wire.
testimonials.name: Marguerite
testimonials.title: Retired Racing Pigeon, Leg Band No. 4471

testimonials.quote: The nest-readiness score said he was a nine. He was a six. But I respected that a number existed at all.
testimonials.name: Two-Toes
testimonials.title: Statue Resident, Central Library Colony

demo_screen_concept: A Hinge-style single-profile browsing screen for a pigeon named Bartholomew. A vertical scroll of profile photos (portrait, on-the-ledge, mid-strut in a plaza) interleaved with three answered prompt cards ('The ledge I call home is...', 'My ideal Sunday is...', 'A green flag I look for is...'). Each individual photo and prompt has its own heart button so you can like one specific thing, plus a 'send a coo' comment field that opens a small composer. A verified-coo checkmark sits by the name and a nest-readiness meter reads 8/10. Bottom bar: a large skip (X) and a large like (heart-stamp) button. Liking anything fires a rubber-stamp animation and a mock 'coo sent' toast. All data is fake and client-side.

design_direction.style: Risograph two-ink print zine, warm urban-newsprint mode. The whole page is treated like a stapled city zine run off a two-color risograph duplicator: a warm recycled-newsprint ground carries a faint halftone dot grain and a subtle off-register overprint feel, as if the blue and coral inks landed a hair out of alignment. Only two spot inks exist. One risograph federal-blue does the wordmark, headlines, body ink, rules, halftone illustration fills, and all structure. One risograph fluoro-coral is rationed strictly for the single primary CTA, the live 'like' heart affordance, the active toggle, and the round 'designed to be deleted' rubber stamp, and appears almost nowhere else. Bricolage Grotesque sets the wordmark and headlines in chunky, slightly irregular poster grotesque, like a hand-set zine cover; DM Sans carries calm caption-weight body copy. Every figure that pretends to be relationship telemetry (coo-match percentage, nest-readiness score, homing radius in wingbeats, tier prices) is set in DM Mono with tabular figures so it reads like a printed classified listing, not a marketing number. Surfaces are flat spot-ink blocks with thick 2-3px ink keylines, hard registration crosses in the margins, and blocky halftone panels. Motion is print-like and cheap: cards snap in with a 2px ink-registration jitter, the like heart presses in like a stamp hitting paper, halftone panels shift a hair on hover. No gradients beyond the flat halftone dot texture, no soft blur, no glassmorphism, no drop-shadow marketing cards, no rounded clay, no dark mode. Reads like a scrappy but genuinely well-funded relationship platform that has been pointed, with total seriousness, at a bird that already mates for life and did not ask for an app.

fonts.body: 'DM Sans', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: Consumer dating / relationship app landing page with a single-profile browsing demo
design_direction.motion_vocab: Print-press micro-motion: 2px ink-registration jitter on entrance, rubber-stamp press-in on the like heart (scale 1.0 to 0.9 and back with a hard settle), halftone panels nudging a hair on hover, toasts snapping in like a stamped receipt. Restrained, cheap, mechanical. Respects reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Big off-register overprint headline in federal blue with the wordmark 'Loft' top-left next to a small halftone pigeon mark. A single bold halftone pigeon portrait anchors the right of the hero. A round coral 'designed to be deleted' rubber stamp overlaps a corner at a slight rotation. Registration crosshairs sit in two margins. The one coral element in the hero is the 'Start roosting' CTA. Flat two-ink throughout, thick blue keylines framing the hero block.

images.rationale: Risograph two-ink style rewards bold halftone illustration, so the budget went to a hero pigeon portrait plus a 3-photo profile gallery for the demo (Bartholomew's Loft profile: head-on portrait, on-the-ledge, mid-strut). Testimonial headshots skipped intentionally; the three named pigeons keep the two-ink monogram/stamp treatment so the accent stays rationed. All four images cleared the vision judge on the first attempt.

items.prompt: Two-color risograph portrait of a dignified city pigeon in three-quarter view, federal blue #2947C4 + fluoro coral #F0426E on newsprint cream #F2ECDC, coarse halftone dots, off-register overprint, negative space on the left for text, no text/letters/numbers/logos/UI.
Two-color risograph head-on dating-profile portrait of a plump city pigeon, blue + coral on newsprint, halftone dots, off-register overprint, plain background, no text/letters/numbers/logos/UI.

Two-color risograph illustration of a city pigeon perched on a weathered concrete ledge with a city skyline behind, blue + coral on newsprint, halftone dots, off-register overprint, no text/letters/numbers/logos/UI.
Two-color risograph illustration of a city pigeon mid-strut across a stone plaza, chest puffed, blue + coral on newsprint, halftone dots, off-register overprint, no text/letters/numbers/logos/UI.
